rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope before use in AGREE contracts.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning helper declarations with the types of their expressions.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE contracts to avoid resolution errors.
Ensure AGREE scalar type names match AADL conventions (int, real, bool) to prevent reference errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid component type or implementation scopes with proper annex syntax.
Guarantee expressions must have well-defined types; avoid <error> types by resolving root causes.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the model's configured dependencies.
Do not reference implementation-only subcomponents in component type contracts.
